editorHome.title: Local Claude, Cursor & Codex
editorHome.subtitle: Browse and edit local Claude/Cursor/Codex files, plus keep anonymous drafts in a separate workspace.
editorHome.selectFile: Pick a file on the left to preview or edit
editorHome.selectOrCreate: Select a file or create a new anonymous draft
editorHome.missingDir: This directory does not exist yet (install or run the tool once).
editorHome.editableWarning: Edits here change local files directly.

imports.title: Imports
imports.empty: Drag files to the window to import
imports.selectOrDrop: Select a file or drag one into the window

externalFiles.title: External Files
externalFiles.subtitle: Keep a local-only working set of files you opened from your device.
externalFiles.selectFile: Open a file with GitMemo to start tracking it here
externalFiles.localOnlyHint: This list stays on this device and is not synced to Git.
externalFiles.fileMissing: This file is no longer available at its original path.
externalFiles.editingLive: Edits here write directly to the original file.
externalFiles.removeConfirm: Remove this file from the external files list?
externalFiles.clearAllConfirm: Remove all files from the external files list?

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.missingHint: The original file for this favorite no longer exists.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.openCommitPage: Open this commit on the remote host
dashboard.emptyGuideDesc: Write a note or chat with Claude Code / Cursor / Codex — content will appear here automatically.
dashboard.emptyGuideMobileDesc: After syncing a remote repository, you can view all content on mobile and record new notes.
dashboard.cliCardTitle: Unlock AI workflows with the CLI
dashboard.cliCardDesc: The CLI connects GitMemo to terminals and AI tools so Claude, Cursor, and Codex can save and reuse your knowledge.
dashboard.cliCardCommandCopied: Install command copied. Run it in your terminal.

notes.enterToSave: Enter to save, Shift+Enter for newline
notes.useInputAbove: Use the input above to create one
notes.docsHint: Use the title + content above, or create via CLI: gitmemo manual
notes.selectOrCreate: Select a file or create a new note

clipboard.title: Clipboard
clipboard.autoCapture: Copy text (20+ chars) or files to auto-capture
clipboard.selectToView: Select a clip to view full content
clipboard.deleteConfirm: Delete this clip from saved history?

search.emptyTitle: Full-text search across all your data
search.mobileEmptyHint: Type a keyword and press Enter

plans.emptyDesc: Plans from the remote repository will appear here after sync.
plans.deleteSourceConfirm: Also delete the original plan in Claude/Cursor? Default is No.

settings.title: Settings
settings.launchAtLoginDesc: Start GitMemo when you log in
settings.controlCopyPasteDesc: Make Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V behave like Cmd+C and Cmd+V in GitMemo
settings.importFileSizeLimitDesc: Control the per-file import limit, from {0} to {1}
settings.dataDirDesc: Where your notes and conversations are stored
settings.remoteTokenHint: Mobile HTTPS sync needs an access token. On GitHub, create a fine-grained personal access token and grant Contents: Read and write for your GitMemo data repository. On Gitee/GitLab, create a personal/access token with read and write access to the target repository. Copy the generated token here. Existing tokens are kept; entering a new token replaces it.
settings.mobileRemoteSummary: Mobile uses the HTTPS URL and access token for sync. To replace the token, edit this remote and enter a new token.
settings.remoteGuide: 1. Create a private repo on GitHub/GitLab  2. Paste the SSH URL above  3. Add your SSH public key to the repo's Deploy Keys (with write access)
settings.syncLogsDesc: View detailed reasons for recent sync failures
settings.mobileGitSpikeDesc: Check mobile Git sync using an HTTPS token.
settings.mobileGitSpikeMissingInput: Enter a remote repository URL and token
settings.mobileDiagnosticDefaultDetail: Fetch and push permission checks passed
settings.claudeIntegrationDesc: Auto-save conversations & sync plans via CLAUDE.md
settings.cliCapabilityDesc: Adds editor setup, Codex/Claude session capture, MCP tools, terminal notes, and Git maintenance commands.
settings.cliCommandCopied: Install command copied. Run it in your terminal.
settings.localDirsDesc: Browse and edit local Claude/Cursor/Codex folders, plus manage anonymous drafts in a separate workspace.
settings.shortcutsDesc: Click a shortcut, then press the new key combination.
settings.shortcutInvalid: Press a shortcut with a key and at least one modifier, or use an F-key.
settings.shortcutGlobalSearchDesc: Show GitMemo and focus search from anywhere
settings.shortcutFindDesc: Find in the current document preview
settings.shortcutEditDesc: Edit the selected note or conversation
settings.shortcutDeleteDesc: Remove the selected note, plan, or import
settings.shortcutRefreshDesc: Refresh the current list and selected file
settings.shortcutFavoriteDesc: Add or remove the selected item from favorites
settings.shortcutSplitPreviewDesc: Toggle split editor preview for the selected file
settings.shortcutCopyDesc: Copy the selected clipboard record content
settings.shortcutMoreActionsDesc: Open the selected file's more actions menu
settings.shortcutGlobalSearch: Cmd+Shift+G: Show GitMemo and focus search
settings.shortcutFind: Cmd/Ctrl+F: Find in current document preview
settings.shortcutEditDelete: Cmd/Ctrl+E edit, Cmd/Ctrl+Delete remove selected note/plan
settings.proxyDesc: Proxy for updates and network requests

dropzone.title: Handle dropped files
dropzone.chooseMode: Choose whether to open the original file directly or import it into GitMemo-managed storage.
dropzone.dropToChoose: Drop files to choose a mode
dropzone.dragHint: You can open a file in place or let GitMemo take over and sync it.
dropzone.singleFileHint: Open keeps the original file in place. Import copies it into GitMemo.
dropzone.multiFileHint: Import supports multiple files. Open works one file at a time.
dropzone.openDesc: Preview or edit the original file directly, without moving it into GitMemo storage.
dropzone.importDesc: Copy the file into GitMemo-managed storage so it can be organized and synced.
dropzone.routeHint: Imported files are still auto-routed to notes, clips, code, docs, or other matching destinations.
dropzone.sizeLimit: Single imported files are limited to {0}; larger files are rejected.
dropzone.openSingleOnly: Open supports one file at a time. Use import to manage multiple files.

common.copyPath: Copy full path on this device

claudeConfig.emptyHint: Click 'Sync to Git' to sync config

privacy.title: Clipboard Privacy
privacy.subtitle: Before enabling clipboard capture
privacy.point1: All captured content is stored locally in your ~/.gitmemo/ Git repository
privacy.point2: Content shorter than 20 characters is automatically ignored
privacy.point3: Common API keys and tokens are auto-filtered (sk-*, ghp_*, etc.)
privacy.point4: You can pause capture anytime from the tray menu or this page

onboarding.title: Get Started with GitMemo
onboarding.installDoneDesc: GitMemo is set up and ready to use
onboarding.firstSaveDesc: Talk to Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex first, then type /save where available. Captured sessions will appear in GitMemo.
onboarding.firstSaveNeedEditorDesc: Set up Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex first. Then chat once and capture the session into GitMemo.
onboarding.enableClipboardDesc: Auto-save copied text (20+ chars) to Git
onboarding.connectRemoteDesc: Sync your knowledge to GitHub/GitLab for backup
onboarding.configureEditorDesc: Auto-save AI conversations from Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex

setup.storageDesc: Choose where to store your knowledge
setup.localModeDesc: Store everything on this device. You can add Git remote later.
setup.remoteModeDesc: Push to GitHub/GitLab for backup and multi-device access.
setup.gitUrlHint: SSH URL recommended. You can change this later in Settings.
setup.mobileGitUrlHint: Mobile sync uses an HTTPS repository URL, for example https://github.com/user/repo.git.
setup.mobileTokenHint: The token is stored only in local config and used for mobile Git sync.
setup.mobileTokenGuide: Mobile HTTPS sync needs an access token. On GitHub, create a fine-grained personal access token and grant Contents: Read and write for your GitMemo data repository. On Gitee/GitLab, create a personal/access token with read and write access to the target repository. Copy the generated token here.
setup.mobileHttpsRequired: Mobile currently supports HTTPS repository URLs only.
setup.sshSelectDesc: Pick an existing key for this remote, or generate a new one for GitMemo.
setup.sshKeyEncryptedReason: This private key requires a passphrase, so GitMemo cannot use it for background sync. Generate a dedicated GitMemo key instead.
setup.onlyEncryptedSshKeysFound: All discovered SSH keys require a passphrase. GitMemo background sync cannot prompt for one, so generate a new dedicated key.
setup.noSshKeysFound: No usable SSH public keys were found yet. Generate one now or check your ~/.ssh directory.
setup.editorsDesc: Select editors to auto-save AI conversations. You can change this later.
setup.cursorDesc: Auto-save conversations via Cursor rules + MCP
setup.codexDesc: Capture native Codex session logs with gitmemo capture
setup.skipEditorsHint: No editor selected — you can enable integrations later in Settings
setup.pleaseWait: Creating directories, initializing Git, configuring integrations...
setup.mobilePleaseWait: Creating directories, initializing Git, syncing remote data...
setup.initLongRunningHint: If the remote repository is large or the network is slow, this step may take tens of seconds. Keep the app in the foreground.
setup.completeDesc: GitMemo is ready. Start capturing your knowledge.
setup.mobileRemoteErrorDesc: Setup did not complete. Check the HTTPS repository URL, token permissions, and network, then retry.
setup.sshKeyHint: Add this key to your Git hosting provider's Deploy Keys.
setup.sshWriteAccess: Make sure to check 'Allow write access' — push will fail without it.
setup.tipLanguage: Pick your language now; you can switch the interface language later in Settings.
setup.tipStorageLocal: Starting in local-only mode is fine; you can add a remote later for backup and multi-device sync.
setup.tipStorageRemote: SSH is the best default here; if you just want to try GitMemo first, switch back to local mode and add the remote later.
setup.tipMobileStorageRemote: Mobile sync uses an HTTPS token and does not depend on system Git, SSH, or AI editor integrations.
setup.tipEditors: Editor integrations are optional; finish setup first and enable them later in Settings if needed.
setup.tipSettingUp: GitMemo is creating folders, Git config, and editor integrations. The result page will appear automatically.
setup.tipSave: In Claude Code or Cursor, talk to the AI first, then type /save. If you run /save before any real conversation, there is nothing useful to save.
